chapters.title: The ubiquitous power that humans have exercised over Earth is why our time is referred to as the Anthropocene era
chapters.text:
The human experiment is now at the mercy of ecological destruction and technological hubris. For several decades now, the choices we have been making concerning the planet drives us further down the rabbit hole. At the same time, some believe that the world is getting better at reducing extreme poverty and child mortality rate; the progressive decline of our civilization remains a threat to our collective existence.

The disappearance of one kind of threat has given rise to another type. In the same way that an individual can grow in strength, size, wealth, and intelligence for many years and then be struck down by cancer, our civilization can also meet a similar fate. The current distribution of wealth and power in our society makes coping with environmental challenges and threats to our culture difficult, if not impossible.

Human ingenuity is proof that we can rise to the challenge and tackle environmental threats.

Yet, there is hope. Technological advancements and people with the sheer will to stand up to the world powers who are not ready to do the needful for selfish reasons encourage the battle to be over. The rest of this piece is designed to honestly present the facts and the need to engage the situation. Then, perhaps, our planet can still be saved.

chapters.title: Earth, man’s only home, is in imminent danger because of global warming
chapters.text:
Global warming is hardly a new topic in our world today. There are tons of books and research papers that warn us of its staggering growth and disastrous effects. However, some people think global warming is somewhere distant and uncertain, like the threat of an alien invasion. Global warming is here, undoing the planet from the inside out.

In fact, there are half as many wild animals on the planet as there were in 1970, an awesome and mostly unnoticed silencing. ~ Bill McKibben

The figures speak for themselves: in the last 30 years, humans have witnessed the hottest 20 years ever recorded. This insane heat has rendered one-third of the Earth’s landmass unable to support animal life.

Plant-based energy, on which all animals ultimately depend, has been on a gradual decline. Even more, we only have half the number of wildlife animals that were existent in 1970. Rising temperatures had also made the earth susceptible to dangerous heat waves, wildfires, and droughts.

Global warming has triggered conflicts and forced people to migrate from their homes.

These problems affect human lives, collectively and individually. For instance, drought in Syria triggered economic instability, one of the major causes of the Syrian Civil War. In addition, Syrian refugees who migrated to other European countries live in fear as the native population considers them security and economic threats.

Coastal regions such as Texas have experienced an increase in atmospheric humidity by 3 to 5%. This contributed to the most devastating rainstorm in US history — Hurricane Harvey with 34 trillion gallons of rainwater, enough to fill 26,000 stadiums. The city of Houston sank by a couple of centimeters under this enormous weight.

This is just the beginning.

chapters.title: Global warming can end human life and almost all other forms of life on Earth
chapters.text:
Many people had heard about the ocean’s rising temperatures. What they don’t know is the terrifying effect. If the oceans keep heating up, by 2100, phytoplankton, the organisms that produce two-thirds of Earth’s oxygen supply, will be unable to function correctly. All animal life, including humans, on Earth, depends on oxygen. With the loss of two – thirds of the earth’s supply, death is certain.

If global warming continues, 2100 might be the year of human extinction.

Rising temperatures also expose life to health risks. Buried under the ice cap at the Arctic Circle are dangerous microbes and viruses that could trigger a pandemic outbreak of illnesses.

In 2016, a heat-wave in Siberia freed the carcass of an infected reindeer trapped under the permafrost. Anthrax from the corpse seeped into nearby streams and rivers, infecting about 2,000 reindeer and humans in turn. A 12-year-old boy was infected and died as a result.

Climate change has become such a familiar term that we tend to read past it — it's part of our mental furniture, like urban sprawl or gun violence. ~ Bill McKibben

Scientists believe there is more where that anthrax came from. The permafrost is a perfect vault for viruses and microbes as it is cold, dark, and has no oxygen supply. Beneath its icy surface are deadly fragments of the Spanish flu, smallpox, and the bubonic plague.

Global warming is a threat to both humans and animals.

Furthermore, as temperatures rise, large chunks of ice break free and float into the ocean. This extra weight in the sea will bend the Earth’s crust, leading to a flurry of volcanic activities. Think landslides, earthquakes, and tsunamis. Scientists are already seeing the seismic activities caused by rising ocean temperatures in Alaska and Greenland.

Even if these events do not occur, only one of them is enough to create worldwide instability and put the fate of humankind and the world at large in jeopardy. But what if these do not occur at all? Does that mean the Earth is safe?

chapters.title: Arable and habitable land is steadily being lost globally due to global warming
chapters.text:
The current effects of global warming are still a cause for alarm. One of which is the loss of arable land. Global warming causes drought on the one hand and flooding on the other. People end up migrating from places that experience these conditions. The result would be more animals and humans trying to survive on a shrinking area of land.

Food production would be affected — for instance, corn production. Corn requires a particular type of climate and crop to grow. The loss of arable land would also mean the loss of lands that can grow corn. Already, corn production is speculated to reduce by half by 2050.

Furthermore, rising ocean levels also mean the loss of land itself to the ocean. Coastal cities will be hit as 2% of Earth’s land will be lost to the sea. If care is not taken, megacities such as New York and Shanghai will be lost forever. There will be significant economic loss and displacement of a large population.

Different consequences of global warming lead to more decline in economic health.

The displaced population will head inland to meet inhabitable areas due to drought and dangerous heat waves. For example, in 2016, primary city residents in Iran and Pakistan had to endure a record-setting 129 degrees Fahrenheit. This would be the fate of almost 2 billion people living in that area, including India. This will disrupt social relationships and communications.

chapters.title: Global warming is a threat to our social lives
chapters.text:
The mass migration of Syrians into European countries triggered the rise of right-wing extremism. Native populations were displeased from what seemed like an invasion of their homeland. Global warming will create even more problems for immigrants and natives. The International Organization for Migration predicts there could be as many as one billion climate refugees by 2050!

Global warming will result in many more people becoming refugees if left unchecked.

The US military worries that mass migration of people would destabilize whole regions, making security almost impossible. Fear of losing their homes and other resources by natives would trigger climate-fueled conflicts.

The exponential increase in refugees will eventually create tension between natives and immigrants.

This security risk adds to potential loss of coastal megacities, drought, decrease in food production, and mass migration. The result is a seething cauldron waiting to explode. Rough estimates put the potential loss at a staggering $535 trillion. This does not cover possible loss of lives and suffering.

Global warming will force people to leave their homes and countries for better regions.

Did you know? According to NASA, most of the warming — of the earth — occurred in the past 40 years, with the seven most recent years being the warmest.

chapters.title: Tampering with the human gene is the second threat to the human race
chapters.text:
Climate change is only one of the three catastrophic threats to the human race. The second is genetic engineering.

Genetic engineering is not something out of a sci-fi movie; it is a part of our present-day reality. The discovery of Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats (CRISPR) means human genes are now editable. On the bright side, it offers a chance to end genetic disorders, such as Down syndrome.

Genetic engineering has both serious positive and negative consequences on the human race.

Using CRISPR, parents can alter the genes of their children before they are born. For example, they can make them smarter, stronger, and more attractive. Because this is an expensive procedure, it would be reserved for the rich alone. Generations born in this way would make up the “GenRich” class.

Those who cannot afford the procedure will continue to give birth to natural babies — naturals. With each generation, the gap between the GenRich class and the naturals will expand until interbreeding between the two categories is impossible, thereby becoming two different species.

The GenRich will occupy the top strata of society due to their enhanced qualities, and the naturals will be condemned to feeding on bones. This subordination could begin the end of humankind as we know it.

chapters.title: Life on another planet is not the answer to the threats of global warming, genetic engineering, and AI
chapters.text:
Science fiction movies and books often sell the idea of the human race surviving on another planet. Unfortunately, while this might be possible as fantasy, it is nearly impossible to achieve.

First, space is cruel and unforgiving. The closest habitable planet in our solar system, Mars, is separated from us by dangerous radioactive cosmic rays. Getting through those rays would be an accomplishment in itself.

Life in space is not a viable option for humans. Earth is humanity’s best option.

Even if one lands safely on Mars, one would be condemned to live in underground bunkers. Why? Mars’ surface area is not suitable for human life. It goes without saying that if humans refuse to live in underground bunkers on Earth, they definitely would not want to do so on Mars.

The next option is to seek a planet whose surface land area is well suited to human life. The closest could probably be in a solar system called Trappist. You would need to travel a distance of 39 light-years to reach that solar system. The fastest space object ever designed on earth was 100 times faster than a bullet. Even at that neck-breaking speed, you’ll have to spend 180,000 years traveling in space.

Our current space transport systems cannot transport humans to other promising solar systems.

The third and only reliable option is to find a habitable planet extremely close to us. Luckily, there is one. It has the right amount of air and other human resources. We only need to save it from destruction. It’s called Earth.

chapters.title: Preserving the human race is simpler than you might think
chapters.text:
Maintaining human life on earth requires only a few steps. These steps, albeit simple, have full implications that would improve all humans’ current and prospects.

Earth can be saved with simple, committed steps. These include reduction of carbon emission, regulation of genetic engineering, and kill-switches for artificial intelligence.

Genetic engineering would require worldwide uniform legislation to govern its application. That simply means countries worldwide must agree on what genetic engineering can be used for and what it cannot be used for. So, for instance, it would be illegal all over the world to create enhanced babies. The legislation must be uniform to ensure everyone abides by the rules.

Regarding global warming, we simply have to cut down our carbon emissions. To do so, we must use alternative renewable power sources instead of burning fossil fuels. Alternative renewable power sources include the sun (solar energy), wind, and hydroelectric power.

The threat of artificial intelligence can be dealt with by introducing a fail-switch into every design. Think of it as a shutdown button to prevent an AI system from being too bright and posing a viable threat to human lives.

If we can initiate these simple steps, we would not need to look for other planets to migrate to. Instead, the earth would be perfect for us.

chapters.title: The biggest and final threat to planet earth is humanity itself — or some part of it
chapters.text:
Scientists are already working on fail-safes on artificial intelligence systems all over the world. Countries around the world can easily create and implement laws regulating the use of genetic engineering. The cost of producing power from alternative sources is cheap and constantly falling. For instance, solar energy costs just 30 cents per watt.

Despite all these, the future of humanity still looks bleak. Why? Because humans are the chief threats, preventing corrective measures from taking place. How?

Humans, the rich and powerful specifically, benefit a lot from the status quo at the expense of our planet and humanity. Using their influences and the power of corporations they control, they can initiate projects, change general impressions, and influence government policies.

For instance, companies in the fossil fuel industry have been trying to curb knowledge of global warming. However, large oil companies, such as Exxon in the 1970s, know that global warming is caused by carbon emissions, which is a direct result of using their products.

Exxon took precautions such as raising the heights of its oil drilling platforms to match the future rise in sea levels. However, this knowledge was not made public until a decade later. They not only withheld the information, but they also spread misinformation to the general public.

Humans have engaged in information cover-ups and misinformation to hide the truth about global warming.

The company took the recommendations from global warming research and on the contrary initiated grand scale deceptive publicity. The goal was to plant doubt in the hearts of the public and politicians on the reality of global warming. The plan worked, and its effects are still being felt today. According to a poll in 2017, more than 30 years later, almost 90% of Americans do not know scientists have reached a consensus on climate change.

A world without death is a world without time, and that in turn is a world without meaning, at least human meaning. ~ Bill McKibben

Going against large corporations and wealthy influential individuals will require a great deal of committed and concentrated efforts. These entities have invested billions into their project and, as such, will not easily back down. However, if we are going to save our planet and ourselves in the process, this is a fight that must be fought — and won!

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Earth is an incredibly hospitable planet. The most inhospitable cubic meter of the Earth’s surface is more gracious than the most appealing corner of Mars or Jupiter. There is life everywhere. From the Sahara Desert to Mount Everest, there is the possibility of breathing natural air. In addition to sustaining life, the Earth is also beautiful. The mountains and ice and forest and ocean continue to play out beautiful scenes before us. From the dew that hangs on stalks of grass to the highest human habitation — the Rongbuk Monastery in Tibet — there is an enjoyable movie being played by nature.

Humans hold all the cards. We can decide to save or destroy this beautiful and life-sustaining planet. Of all creatures, we are the only creatures capable of self-restraint. We can choose not to do what we are capable of doing. A bee cannot decide not to make honey. A bird must fly whether it likes it or not.

Put simply, between ecological destruction and technological hubris, the human experiment is now in question. ~ Bill McKibben

We can choose to replace our roofs with solar panels. We can also stop engineering the genes of our children. With grace, we can accept that each of us has a moment and a place as much as we can keep ourselves alive as digitally preserved consciousness. Whether we will make the right choices is unknown. Currently, we are faltering. The relentless rise in temperature is proof of this. But we can turn things around. With non-violent tools and the fundamental idea of human solidarity, things could change.

Try this

We can save the planet earth through collective effort, as well as through individual responsibility.

• Check your carbon footprint

• Plant a tree

• Buy and use environmentally friendly products

Read it till the end?
